lines: Welcome. [long pause] You've made it to the end of another day. [long pause] Whatever it held... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] whatever it asked of you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] it's behind you now. [long pause] And you are here. [long pause] In your bed. [long pause] In the quiet. [long pause] With nothing left to do.

Take a moment to settle in. [long pause] Adjust your blankets if you need to... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] pull them up or push them down until the temperature feels right. [long pause] Shift your pillow. [long pause] Move your body until you find the position that feels like... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] yes. [long pause] This is where I'll be.

Notice the weight of the blanket on your body. [long pause] That gentle, even pressure across your chest, your stomach, your legs. [long pause] It's such a simple thing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and yet the body responds to it. [long pause] The weight says... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you are contained. [long pause] You are covered. [long pause] You can stop bracing against the world for a few hours.

Now notice the surface beneath you. [long pause] The mattress accepting your weight. [long pause] Not pushing back... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not giving way... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just holding. [long pause] The pillow cradling the back of your head. [long pause] The sheets, smooth or textured, against the skin of your arms and legs. [long pause] You are surrounded by surfaces that are designed to support you. [long pause] That's all they do. [long pause] They hold you up so you can let go.

Let your jaw unclench. [long pause] Let your teeth part just slightly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a millimeter of space between your upper and lower jaw. [long pause] Let your tongue drop from the roof of your mouth and rest behind your lower teeth. [long pause] Soften the tiny muscles around your eyes... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the ones that squint at screens and furrow at problems. [long pause] These are the places we hold tension without knowing it. [long pause] Release them now.

Bring your attention to your breathing. [long pause] Don't change it yet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just observe. [long pause] The air coming in through your nose, slightly cool. [long pause] The air leaving, slightly warmer. [long pause] The chest rising... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and falling. [long pause] The belly expanding... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and settling.

Now, lengthen the exhale. [long pause] In through the nose... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And out through the mouth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven. [long pause] The exhale longer than the inhale. [long pause] This is not a trick. [long pause] This is physiology. [long pause] The longer exhale activates the vagus nerve, which tells your nervous system... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you are safe. [long pause] You can stand down. [long pause] The threat is not real.

Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And out... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] letting the breath leave like water pouring slowly from a glass... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven.

One more cycle. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out, slow and steady, all the way to empty. [long pause] Good. [long pause] Let your breath return to whatever feels natural. [long pause] It will find its own pace.
"Be anxious for nothing, but in everything by prayer and supplication, with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God." Philippians, chapter four, verse six.

Hear this not as a rebuke. [long pause] Paul wrote these words from a prison cell... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] chained to a Roman guard, uncertain of his future. [long pause] He was not a man unfamiliar with anxiety. [long pause] This is an invitation from someone who knew what it cost to trust, not a lecture from someone who never worried. [long pause] Tonight, we accept the invitation gently.

What are you carrying right now? [long pause] [long pause] Name it silently. [long pause] A concern. [long pause] A task left undone. [long pause] A conversation that went wrong or hasn't happened yet. [long pause] A health worry. [long pause] A financial pressure. [long pause] Something about someone you love. [long pause] Something about yourself.

You don't have to solve it tonight. [long pause] You don't even have to set it down completely... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] that's a lot to ask. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] set it beside the bed. [long pause] Put it on the nightstand, next to your phone. [long pause] It can be there in the morning. [long pause] It will wait. [long pause] Problems are patient that way.
"And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus."

Guard. [long pause] The Greek word is phroureo... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a military term. [long pause] It means to garrison. [long pause] To post sentries around. [long pause] The peace of God stands watch tonight so that you don't have to. [long pause] Your mind can stop patrolling the perimeter. [long pause] You are off duty.

We're going to move through the body now. [long pause] This is a practice called Yoga Nidra, which means yogic sleep. [long pause] It is one of the oldest relaxation techniques known... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] thousands of years old. [long pause] The method is simple. [long pause] I'll name parts of your body, one by one, and all you do is move your awareness there. [long pause] You don't need to relax that part. [long pause] You don't need to do anything to it. [long pause] Simply notice it, the way you'd notice a room you're walking through. [long pause] The noticing itself is the practice.
If you fall asleep during this... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] that's fine. [long pause] That's the point. [long pause] Wherever you stop, the work is done.

Begin with the right hand. [long pause] Move your awareness to the right thumb... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] feel it from the inside. [long pause] The index finger... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the middle finger... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the ring finger... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the little finger.
The palm of the right hand. [long pause] Notice whatever is there... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] warmth, tingling, nothing at all. [long pause] The back of the right hand.

The right wrist... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right forearm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right elbow, that hard knot of bone... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right upper arm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right shoulder.

The right side of the chest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right side of the ribcage, expanding and contracting with the breath... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right side of the waist... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right hip, heavy against the mattress.

The right thigh, the front and back of it... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right knee... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right shin... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right calf... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right ankle, that delicate joint... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the top of the right foot... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the sole of the right foot... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the toes of the right foot. [long pause] Big toe... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] second toe... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] third... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] fourth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] little toe.

Now the left hand. [long pause] Awareness to the left thumb... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the index finger... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the middle finger... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the ring finger... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the little finger.
The palm of the left hand... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the back of the left hand.

The left wrist... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left forearm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left elbow... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left upper arm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left shoulder.

The left side of the chest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left side of the ribcage... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left side of the waist... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left hip.

The left thigh... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left knee... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left shin... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left calf... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left ankle... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the top of the left foot... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the sole of the left foot... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the toes of the left foot. [long pause] Big toe... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] second... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] third... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] fourth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] little toe.

Now the back of the body. [long pause] The right shoulder blade... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left shoulder blade... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the whole upper back, broad and flat against the bed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the middle back... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the lower back, the curve of the spine... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right buttock... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left buttock... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the backs of the thighs... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the backs of the knees... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the calves resting against the sheets.

Now the front. [long pause] The top of the head, the crown... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the forehead, wide and smooth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right temple... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left temple... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right eyebrow... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left eyebrow... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the space between the eyebrows. [long pause] Rest here for a moment. [long pause] This point, the space between the eyebrows, is sometimes called the third eye... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a place of deep stillness in many traditions.

The right eyelid... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left eyelid... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] so thin, so light, barely resting over the eyes. [long pause] The right eye behind the lid... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left eye. [long pause] Still. [long pause] Not searching for anything.

The right ear... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left ear... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] listening to this voice, and to the silence between the words. [long pause] The right cheek... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left cheek... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the nose, the bridge and the tip... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the upper lip... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the lower lip... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the chin.

The throat... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] open, soft... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the right collarbone... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the left collarbone... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the chest, the sternum... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the heart center, where the ribs meet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the navel... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the lower abdomen... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the pelvis.

Now, the whole right arm. [long pause] Feel it as one unit, from shoulder to fingertip. [long pause] Just one thing. [long pause] The whole left arm. [long pause] The whole right leg, from hip to toes. [long pause] The whole left leg. [long pause] The whole torso... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] front, back, and sides. [long pause] The whole head... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] face, skull, jaw, scalp.

The whole body. [long pause] Awareness of the whole body lying here, breathing. [long pause] One single field of sensation. [long pause] Not parts anymore... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just the whole.
"The peace of God, which surpasses all understanding... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "

Let that phrase settle into the body you've just moved through. [long pause] Each part that was named is now known, acknowledged, at rest. [long pause] Peace doesn't require understanding. [long pause] It doesn't require effort. [long pause] It was placed here, in you, by someone who guards your heart while you sleep.

Now we practice paired opposites. [long pause] The nervous system releases its deepest tension when it experiences one state, then its opposite, and then neither. [long pause] This is how the body remembers what neutral feels like.

Feel your body becoming heavy. [long pause] Starting with your arms... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] your right arm is heavy, dense, sinking into the mattress. [long pause] Your left arm is heavy. [long pause] Both arms made of warm stone. [long pause] Your legs are heavy... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] your right leg pressing down into the bed, your left leg pressing down. [long pause] Your whole body is heavy. [long pause] As though gravity has doubled. [long pause] As though you are slowly sinking into the earth itself... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] through the mattress, through the floor, into warm, dark, quiet earth. [long pause] Every muscle surrendered to the weight.
Feel the heaviness in your hands. [long pause] In your jaw. [long pause] In your eyelids. [long pause] Everywhere... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] pure weight. [long pause] Pure surrender.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] slowly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the heaviness lifts. [long pause] And in its place comes lightness. [long pause] Your body begins to float. [long pause] Your arms lift slightly from the mattress... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] your legs feel as though they're suspended in warm water. [long pause] Your chest rises. [long pause] You are weightless... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] buoyant... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] held in air or water or light... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] something that has no name but knows how to carry you. [long pause] Effortless. [long pause] Floating. [long pause] The boundaries of your body soften. [long pause] You are light... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] lifting... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] free.
Float here for a moment. [long pause] No effort required. [long pause] No gravity. [long pause] No weight.

Now feel warmth. [long pause] A spreading warmth from the center of your chest outward. [long pause] Not a surface warmth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a deep warmth, from the core. [long pause] It radiates through your ribs, down your arms, through your stomach, into your hips, along your legs, to the soles of your feet and the tips of your fingers. [long pause] The warmth of sunlight absorbed deep into the body. [long pause] The warmth of safety. [long pause] The warmth of being held by something that will not let go.

And now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] coolness. [long pause] A gentle coolness on your forehead, like a compress of cool water. [long pause] Coolness on your palms... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] on the soles of your feet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] on the back of your neck. [long pause] Like stepping into the shade of an old tree on a warm afternoon. [long pause] Fresh. [long pause] Clean. [long pause] The body sighing with relief. [long pause] Cool.

And now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] neither heavy nor light. [long pause] Neither warm nor cool. [long pause] Neither floating nor sinking. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] here. [long pause] The body at rest in the truest sense... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not pulled in any direction. [long pause] Not becoming anything. [long pause] Not resisting anything. [long pause] Simply existing. [long pause] The peace that surpasses understanding feels like this. [long pause] It is not a state you create. [long pause] It is what remains when everything else is released.

See a place now. [long pause] It comes to you on its own... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] don't force it. [long pause] A place where you have felt safe. [long pause] Maybe it's a real place... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a room, a landscape, a chair by a window. [long pause] Maybe it's a place that exists only in the imagination... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a garden, a shore, a quiet forest. [long pause] Whatever appears first... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] that is the right place.

Notice the light in this place. [long pause] Is it golden? [long pause] [long pause] Blue? [long pause] [long pause] Dim? [long pause] [long pause] Bright? [long pause] [long pause] Notice what you can hear... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] or whether it's silent. [long pause] Notice the temperature of the air, the feeling of the ground or the chair or the sand beneath you. [long pause] You are here, and you are safe, and nothing can enter this place that does not belong.

This place is guarded. [long pause] The peace that surpasses understanding has posted its sentries at every border. [long pause] Nothing that would disturb your rest can reach you here. [long pause] Not the worries. [long pause] Not the tasks. [long pause] Not the fears. [long pause] Not tonight. [long pause] Tonight, you are garrisoned by peace.

Lord... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] we didn't earn this peace. [long pause] We can't manufacture it or sustain it by effort. [long pause] It comes from you. [long pause] It surpasses everything we know how to do for ourselves. [long pause] Guard our hearts tonight. [long pause] Guard our restless, wandering minds. [long pause] We hand ourselves over to you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not perfectly, not with unshakable faith, but honestly. [long pause] That is enough. [long pause] Amen.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Let the imagery dissolve if it wants to. [long pause] Let the body scan become a distant memory. [long pause] Let the paired opposites fade. [long pause] All that remains is the peace... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and the breathing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and the quiet weight of your body in the bed.
The peace guards you. [long pause] Your heart is quiet. [long pause] Your mind is slowing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] thoughts thinning out like clouds at the end of the day.
Guarded... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] resting... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] garrisoned by a peace you did not earn.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
Nothing to solve. [long pause] Nothing to understand. [long pause] Nothing to do.
Peace... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] surpassing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] understanding.
